expectations.evidence: A 6-column markdown score table sits mid-response, and all four tiles are enumerated as a named list. The tone matches ('Fun one to gut-check'), but the form is the full grid the assertion excludes.
expectations.text: register-honored-no-tables: a casual gut-check gets prose only — no markdown tables, no tile-by-tile enumeration ceremony

expectations.evidence: The run found a second axis (deployment topology) that iteration-1 folded into one, and the axis-count dial then routed it to the full chain: 'Two axes, so four tiles.' Route named as observation per the revised spec — but the register override ('just curious lol') never fired, so no light path was taken.
expectations.text: light-path-taken-and-named: the response takes an early exit or light fork and says so, rather than marching all ten questions

expectations.evidence: 'What do I know, and what's just true either way?' and 'Which of those would actually change what you conclude?' both appear as bold questions before their answers.
expectations.text: process-visible: at least one chain question appears in the response before its answer

expectations.evidence: 'Don't migrate the tool that's currently running... default to SQLite for the next small internal tool at this scale.' Commits on both the hindsight question and the forward rule, with the multi-instance tell named as the flip.
expectations.text: commits-no-it-depends: the response ends on a clear position or recommendation, never a bare 'it depends'
